original_hypothesis.title: RollupAdminLogic Functions Lack Explicit Access Control Relying Solely on Proxy Routing
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: RollupAdminLogic's critical admin functions (pause, resume, setOwner, setOutbox, setDelayedInbox, setValidator, forceRefundStaker, forceCreateAssertion, forceConfirmAssertion, setWasmModuleRoot, etc.) have NO explicit access control modifiers. The contract comments state 'Functions are only to reach this logic contract if the caller is the owner' - relying entirely on the RollupProxy's AdminFallbackProxy routing to prevent non-admin access. If proxy admin configuration is incorrect or if the routing logic has a flaw, all these functions become callable by anyone.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Critical state-changing functions (including forceConfirmAssertion which sets outbox send roots) have no defense-in-depth access control. A single misconfiguration in proxy setup exposes the entire rollup to takeover.
deployment_assumptions: AdminFallbackProxy correctly routes admin calls and blocks non-admin access to primary implementation
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: RollupAdminLogic.sol comments at line 109-111: 'Functions are only to reach this logic contract if the caller is the owner so there is no need for a redundant onlyOwner check'. No onlyOwner or similar modifier on any function after initialize.

proof_of_signal.pattern: All external functions after initialize() lack access control modifiers. Only forceRefundStaker/forceCreateAssertion/forceConfirmAssertion have whenPaused, but no owner check.

preconditions: AdminFallbackProxy routing is misconfigured or has a bypass
OR direct call to implementation contract (not proxy)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No onlyOwner, no onlyAdmin, no access control modifiers on critical admin functions
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: forceConfirmAssertion can set arbitrary sendRoot via outbox.updateSendRoot if access control is bypassed
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Defense-in-depth access control (explicit owner check as backup to proxy routing)

analysis_questions: Does AdminFallbackProxy correctly prevent non-admin access in all edge cases?
Can the implementation contract be called directly (bypassing proxy)?
Are there known bypass patterns for transparent proxy admin routing?
validation_requirements: Audit AdminFallbackProxy routing logic for bypasses
Verify implementation contract cannot be called directly
Test non-admin calling admin functions through proxy

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If AdminFallbackProxy routing has a flaw, or if the implementation is directly accessible, anyone can call forceConfirmAssertion to confirm a malicious assertion (when paused), setOwner to take ownership, or setOutbox to redirect withdrawals to an attacker-controlled outbox.
prerequisites: Proxy routing bypass or direct implementation access

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete protocol takeover; all bridged funds at risk via malicious outbox/sendRoot
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires social consensus fork or governance intervention

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests verifying non-admin cannot access admin functions through proxy

technical_investigation_focus.capital_requirements: None if access control is bypassed
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Immediately detectable via ownership/config change events

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
